mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production signals expanding economic activity, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the rolling 3-year correlation between INDPRO growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is negative or indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate over three months signals a weakening labor market and impending recession, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases >0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period
failure_condition: If positive forward returns occur in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE has risen by 0.5pp or more over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising interest rates accompanied by above-target inflation create restrictive financial conditions that compress equity valuation multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rate exceeds the year-over-year change in CPIAUCSL by at least 1 percentage point
regime_hint: tightening cycle with positive real rate
failure_condition: If the joint signal yields a rank IC above -0.03 over a 2-year rolling window (i.e., no significant negative relationship).

mechanism_narrative: In the early expansion phase when the output gap is negative, robust GDP growth signals a strong recovery that boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: early expansion: output gap negative, capacity utilization below 78%
failure_condition: If the frequency of positive 60-day returns following this trigger in the specified regime is ≤50% or if the median return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail and food services sales signals a sudden consumer spending pullback that forecasts weakening corporate revenues and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If more than 60% of such retail sales drops are followed by positive 20-day returns, or if the mean return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts decline while the federal funds rate is rising, it signals a coordinated housing market downturn that propagates to the broader economy and depresses equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of HOUST is declining and FEDFUNDS has increased in the prior 3 months
failure_condition: If the joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in less than 70% of activated windows over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payroll employment reflects a healthy labor market that supports household income and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the rolling 2-year correlation between month-over-month PAYEMS changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is negative or not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, monetary policy is restrictive enough to slow economic activity and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the proportion of positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% exceeds 50%, or if the median return is not significantly less than zero.
